options.blogdescription: Where great design is brought back to life.

steps.code:
'Case Files', 'post_name' => 'workshop-checklist', 'post_status' => 'publish', 'post_type' => 'page', 'post_content' => '', )); $c = '

The Design Morgue — Case Files

'; $c .= '

Five specimens. Five preventable deaths. Examine each exhibit, diagnose the crime, and attempt resuscitation. You are the forensic designer now.

'; $c .= ''; $c .= '
'; $c .= '

Exhibit A — The Mutilated Logo

'; $c .= '

Cause of death: Excessive whitespace around the logo mark. The brand drowns in its own canvas. Nobody sees the name — they see emptiness.

'; $c .= ''; $c .= ''; $c .= '

Your task: Visit the home page and look at the header — the logo is drowning in whitespace. Then click "Edit Site" in the top bar, select the logo in the header, and replace it with the properly cropped version from the Media Library (search for "Cropped"). Compare how the header looks before and after.

'; $c .= '
'; $c .= '

Exhibit B — The Accent Color Massacre

'; $c .= '

Cause of death: Everything is highlighted. When every element screams for attention, nothing gets it. The eye has nowhere to land.

'; $c .= '

Your task: Visit the home page and count how many elements are red — buttons, headings, everything is competing. Then click "Edit Page" in the top bar, find the buttons and headings using the red accent, and remove it from all but one: the single most important call-to-action. Compare how your eye moves through the page before and after.

'; $c .= '

Hint: to remove the accent, change the button or heading color to the theme default — not to white or transparent. Check the color panel in the block settings sidebar.

'; $c .= '
'; $c .= '

Exhibit C — The Pale Witness

'; $c .= '

Cause of death: A low-resolution image used at full width. The pixels are visible. The brand looks cheap.

'; $c .= '

Your task: Visit the home page and look at the full-width image — zoom in if you can. Notice the visible pixel blocks. Then click "Edit Page" in the top bar, select the full-width image, and replace it with the high-resolution version from the Media Library (search for "HD"). Compare how the brand feels before and after.

'; $c .= '
'; $c .= '

Exhibit D — The Void

'; $c .= '

Cause of death: No breathing room. Text and elements are compressed together, creating visual claustrophobia. The layout communicates panic, not elegance.

'; $c .= '

Your task: Visit the home page and notice how the hero and the bottom section feel compressed — text almost touching the edges, no room to breathe. Then click "Edit Page" in the top bar, select the dark hero group and the light CTA group, and increase the padding to at least 60px top and bottom on each. Compare how the page feels before and after.

'; $c .= '

Hints: try setting the hero and CTA groups to full width using the alignment toolbar. For padding, use the theme spacing presets (look for the slider in the Dimensions panel) instead of typing pixel values — they scale better across screen sizes. For the services section, a wide alignment gives the columns more horizontal room.

'; $c .= '
'; $c .= '

Exhibit E — The Palette Paradox

'; $c .= '

Cause of death: The wrong background for the content type. Dark backgrounds reduce eye strain in long reading sessions. White backgrounds aid legibility for text-heavy layouts. Neither is wrong — but they are not interchangeable.

'; $c .= '

Your task: Visit the home page and take in the overall palette — does the light background feel right for this brand? Then click "Edit Site" and open the Styles panel (the half-circle icon, top right). Switch the background to dark and visit the home page again. Switch back to light. Decide which palette actually serves this content and note why.

'; wp_update_post(array('ID' => $cid, 'post_content' => $c)); // --- Home page with deliberate design crimes --- $hid = wp_insert_post(array( 'post_title' => 'Home', 'post_name' => 'home', 'post_status' => 'publish', 'post_type' => 'page', 'post_content' => '', )); $h = file_get_contents('/wordpress/home-page.html'); $h = str_replace('{{UPLOADS}}', content_url('uploads'), $h); wp_update_post(array('ID' => $hid, 'post_content' => $h)); // Set home page as front page update_option('show_on_front', 'page'); update_option('page_on_front', $hid); // Register images in media library and set site logo $base = WP_CONTENT_DIR . '/uploads/'; $images = array( array('logo-cropped.png', 'VITAL Logo (Cropped)', 'image/png'), array('logo-uncropped.jpg', 'VITAL Logo (Uncropped)', 'image/jpeg'), array('specimen-lowres.jpg', 'VITAL Campaign Shot', 'image/jpeg'), array('specimen-hires.jpg', 'VITAL Campaign Shot HD', 'image/jpeg'), array('banner.jpg', 'Design Morgue Workshop Banner', 'image/jpeg'), ); foreach ($images as $img) { $id = wp_insert_attachment(array( 'post_title' => $img[1], 'post_mime_type' => $img[2], 'post_status' => 'inherit', ), $base . $img[0], 0); if ('logo-uncropped.jpg' === $img[0]) { update_option('site_logo', $id); } } echo 'done';

steps.data:

'&action=edit' ) : admin_url(); $banner = content_url( 'uploads/banner.jpg' ); ?>
Case No. DM-001 — Filed:

The Design Morgue

The specimens have been laid out. Each one died of a preventable condition.

Your task: examine the evidence, identify the cause of death, and attempt resuscitation.

→ Open the Case Files & Begin the Examination

steps.code:
insert( $wpdb->prefix . 'mlw_quizzes', array( 'quiz_name' => 'Design Morgue: Knowledge Check', 'randomness_order' => 2, 'show_score' => 1, 'total_user_tries' => 0, 'ajax_show_correct' => 1, 'require_log_in' => 0, 'user_name' => 2, 'user_comp' => 2, 'user_email' => 2, 'user_phone' => 2, 'comment_section' => 1, 'deleted' => 0, 'quiz_views' => 0, 'quiz_taken' => 0, 'last_activity' => current_time('mysql'), 'submit_button_text' => 'Submit Answers', 'message_before' => 'Test your knowledge. Five questions. One from each exhibit.', 'message_after' => 'You answered %SCORE% out of %TOTAL_QUESTIONS% correctly.', ) ); $quiz_id = $wpdb->insert_id; function dm_q($quiz_id, $text, $answers, $correct_idx) { global $wpdb; $arr = array(); foreach ($answers as $i => $a) { $arr[] = array($a, ($i === $correct_idx) ? 1 : 0, ($i === $correct_idx) ? 1 : 0, ''); } $wpdb->insert( $wpdb->prefix . 'mlw_questions', array( 'quiz_id' => $quiz_id, 'question_name' => $text, 'answer_array' => maybe_serialize($arr), 'correct_answer' => $correct_idx + 1, 'question_type' => 0, 'question_type_new' => '0', 'question_order' => 0, 'comments' => 1, 'question_settings' => maybe_serialize(array('Required' => '1')), 'deleted' => 0, 'deleted_question_bank' => 0, ) ); return $wpdb->insert_id; } $qids = array(); $qids[] = dm_q($quiz_id, 'What goes wrong when a logo file has excessive whitespace around the mark?', array( 'The file size increases unnecessarily', 'The logo appears tiny and weak -- it occupies a fraction of its own canvas', 'The logo becomes hard to print on physical materials', 'The typeface becomes illegible at small sizes', ), 1 ); $qids[] = dm_q($quiz_id, 'What happens when you apply the accent color to every heading, button, and element on a page?', array( 'Users remember the brand color more strongly', 'The page fails accessibility contrast guidelines', 'Every element competes for attention, so nothing gets it', 'The color loses meaning only on dark backgrounds', ), 2 ); $qids[] = dm_q($quiz_id, 'A low-resolution image is displayed at full width. What does this signal to visitors?', array( 'A vintage or film-inspired aesthetic', 'The image is still loading', 'The brand looks cheap -- image quality signals brand quality', 'The photographer used natural light', ), 2 ); $qids[] = dm_q($quiz_id, 'What does tight, compressed padding around content communicate to the viewer?', array( 'Efficiency and information density', 'A minimal, editorial style', 'Visual claustrophobia -- panic, not elegance', 'Faster page loading through reduced CSS', ), 2 ); $qids[] = dm_q($quiz_id, 'Which statement best describes dark and light palettes?', array( 'Dark palettes always feel more premium and should be preferred', 'Light palettes improve legibility across all content types', 'Neither is wrong, but they are not interchangeable -- each serves different content', 'Dark palettes should only be used for tech brands', ), 2 ); // Link questions to quiz via the pages structure QSM uses to build its IN() query $wpdb->update( $wpdb->prefix . 'mlw_quizzes', array('quiz_settings' => maybe_serialize(array('pages' => array($qids)))), array('quiz_id' => $quiz_id) ); // Create published qsm_quiz post and link via meta so QSM shows it as published $quiz_post_id = wp_insert_post(array( 'post_title' => 'Design Morgue: Knowledge Check', 'post_content' => '[mlw_quizmaster quiz=' . $quiz_id . ']', 'post_status' => 'publish', 'post_author' => get_current_user_id(), 'post_type' => 'qsm_quiz', )); add_post_meta($quiz_post_id, 'quiz_id', intval($quiz_id)); // Create dedicated Knowledge Check page $kid = wp_insert_post(array( 'post_title' => 'Knowledge Check', 'post_name' => 'knowledge-check', 'post_status' => 'publish', 'post_type' => 'page', 'post_content' => '', )); $shortcode = '[mlw_quizmaster quiz=' . $quiz_id . ']'; $k = '

Five questions. One from each exhibit. Answer them to confirm what you have learned. If you get any wrong, you can retake the quiz -- correct answers will be shown.

'; $k .= '' . $shortcode . ''; wp_update_post(array('ID' => $kid, 'post_content' => $k)); // Append Knowledge Check link and conclusions to checklist page $page = get_page_by_path('workshop-checklist'); if ($page) { $quiz_url = get_permalink($kid); $q = '
'; $q .= '

Knowledge Check

'; $q .= '

Completed all five exhibits? Head to the Knowledge Check to confirm what you have learned.

'; $q .= '
'; $q .= '

Your Conclusions

'; $q .= '

Close the tab when you are done. The morgue does not keep records — only lessons.

'; wp_update_post(array('ID' => $page->ID, 'post_content' => $page->post_content . $q)); } echo 'quiz done';
